name: wallet-selection-hl-skill
description: Hyperliquid wallet-selection + rating config tuned to surface the RARE genuinely-skilled trader for MANUAL review, NOT to autonomously copy. 'Best' = persistent rank (top-decile in many forward 60d windows), genuine ANTICIPATION (entry timing leads favorable moves rather than reacting to beta), regime-ROBUSTNESS (edge that does not fully invert at the two regime breaks the study window contained), and deflated-Sharpe survival (beats the best-of-N luck hurdle for a 1063-account search). It explicitly targets the 43-account persistent set and the 9-of-12 measurable anticipators from HL_PERSISTENCE_STUDY_2026-06, and ranks WITHIN that cohort. Inverts the usual screen: trailing PnL and leaderboard rank are DEMOTED to red flags (the study proved both buy regime-momentum at the moment it inverts); persistence + anticipation + regime-robustness are the primary axes. A blow-up flag (maxDD, negative study return) is a hard guard, because even within the persistent tail one member blew up -367%.
changelog: 0.1.0 — initial HL persistent-skill config. Adapts the A-F / 0-10 schema of wallet-selection-v0.1.0.json to Hyperliquid metrics. Sanity targets: 0x6d6d7c (Sharpe 3.2, lowest concentration 0.48, maxDD 0.51) and 0x5559da (Sharpe 2.5, maxDD 0.28 — cleanest book) should grade A. 0xc4643e (anticipating, +6.71%/24h edge, topDecile 4/5) should grade A but carry a THIN_MARKET_IMPACT badge (meme/new-listing edge can be own market impact). 0xe4d1fa (qualified persistent then blew up -367%, maxDD 6.4) must AUTO-DISQUALIFY. Leaderboard-only / trailing-PnL-only standouts with low persistence should NOT clear C.

studyConstants._doc: Fixed from the pre-registered study; the regime-break transitions are the load-bearing facts for this philosophy.

eligibility._doc: Hard cutoffs to be SCORED at all. Below these = excluded (not graded F, just unmeasurable). Mirrors the study's usable-account definition.

persistenceCore._doc: PRIMARY AXIS #1. Does the trader hold top-decile rank window over window? topDecileCount = number of the 5 forward windows (w1..w5) in which the account is in the top decile of raw return. The persistent set is defined as >=3. We grade ABOVE that bar to separate consistent skill from threshold-grazers.

topDecileForwardCount._doc: Out of 5 forward windows. 4-5 = exceptional persistence (top of the 43); 3 = persistent-set membership; 2 = proximity (worth surfacing); <2 = not persistent.

forwardRankStability._doc: Spearman-style consistency of the account's within-regime forward-window ranks (computed against the per-window cross-sectional decile cutoffs over the 3 within-regime transitions w1->w2, w2->w3, w4->w5). Higher = rank holds within a regime. Range 0..1 after min-max normalization of the account's mean within-regime percentile.

persistentSetMembership._doc: Bonus signal: is the account in the pre-computed 43? Not required (we also surface proximity cases at topDecile 2), but membership is a strong prior.

subWeights._doc: Intra-category blend weights inside the persistence category (the category's overall weight is in ratingRubric.categories). topDecile carries most; rank-stability the rest. Lifted from the scorer so the blend is config-tweakable.

anticipation._doc: PRIMARY AXIS #2. Does the trader's ENTRY TIMING precede favorable moves (real skill), or merely ride beta (reacting)? Sourced from anticipation.json: the 24h/4h signed forward edge vs random-timing baselines (CI must exclude zero) and the same-day BTC+ETH regression label. Only ~12 of the 43 have adequate fills coverage; the rest are UNMEASURED and scored NEUTRAL (not penalized) so the tool still surfaces them for manual review.

label._doc: Direct from study: 'anticipating' (entries lead), 'reacting/riding' (entries lag/ride beta), 'insufficient-data (anticipating)' (positive but <14d / <100 entries), 'insufficient-data (unclear)' (no usable fills).

edge24hLowerCI._doc: Lower bound of the 95% CI on the 24h entry edge. >0 means genuine timing edge survives the bootstrap. Negative-but-anticipating-at-shorter-horizon handled by edge4hLowerCI fallback.

edge4hLowerCIFallback._doc: For short-horizon anticipators (24h edge muted but 4h edge positive, e.g. 0x84abc0 / 0xae7a0f). Used only when 24h CI does not clear.

thinMarketImpactFlag._doc: An anticipation edge measured mostly on thin meme / new-listing coins can be the trader's OWN market impact, not foresight (study caveat on 0xc4643e: TRUMP/BERA). Flag, do not disqualify; warns the human reviewer.

regimeRobustness._doc: PRIMARY AXIS #3 — the kill-shot the study identified. Rank persistence INVERTS across the two regime breaks (w0->w1, w3->w4). A genuinely skilled trader's forward return should NOT collapse at both breaks. We measure the account's forward return in the two regime-break windows (w1 contains the Oct cascade aftermath; w4 the Feb-Apr downturn) and reward NOT fully inverting. This is what separates skill from a regime-momentum bet wearing a skill costume.

regimeBreakSurvival._doc: Count of the two regime-break windows (w1 and w4 returns) in which the account stayed non-catastrophic (windowReturn > -0.5, i.e. did not lose >50% of avg account value). 2 = robust through both breaks; 1 = survived one; 0 = inverted at both.

worstWindowReturn._doc: Min of the 6 window returns. How deep the single worst 60d window went (fraction of avg account value). Bounds the downside even within the persistent set.

crossRegimeIcSignFlag._doc: Informational: the universe-level w0->w1 IC was -0.26 and w3->w4 -0.06/-0.17. We do NOT select on cross-regime persistence (it inverts); we select on robustness THROUGH it. This field documents that we are aware trailing rank is anti-predictive at breaks.

deflatedSharpe._doc: SECONDARY. Sharpe alone over-selects in a best-of-N search (1063 candidates). We deflate: require the annualized Sharpe (QV estimator from the study) to clear a luck hurdle for the number of independent trials. Approximate deflated-Sharpe: a Sharpe of ~1.0 is roughly the best-of-1063 expected max under the null over ~5 windows, so we set the floor materially above it.

sharpeAnnual._doc: Annualized Sharpe (QV estimator). Median of the 43 is 1.37; 9 accounts >=2.0; 7 <0.5 (lottery-ish even within the set). Floor screens the lottery members OUT.

bestOfNHurdleSharpe._doc: The expected max Sharpe of the best of N pure-luck books over the study horizon. With N=1063 usable accounts and ~5 windows, E[max] ~ 1.0. A passing account must clear this to claim skill rather than search luck.

pnlConcentration._doc: best5StepsShare from the study: share of total PnL coming from the best 5 (~6d) steps. >1.0 means the rest of the year was net negative — a few-bursts lottery profile. Lower = steadier skill. Median of the 43 ~1.22.

blowUpGuard._doc: GUARD (not an axis). Even genuine persistence does not protect a copier from a book that subsequently detonates. One member of the 43 (0xe4d1fa) qualified then blew up -367%. These are tail tripwires.

maxDrawdownFrac._doc: Max drawdown as a fraction of avg account value (study QV estimator). Median of the 43 ~1.32 (i.e. >100% of avg AV — these are high-variance books). We grade tightly and hard-reject the catastrophic tail.

studyPeriodReturnNegative._doc: If the account's full-study return on avg account value is negative, it blew up over the measured year despite qualifying in >=3 windows. Auto-disqualify — this is the 0xe4d1fa case.

ratingRubric._doc: Per-category letter grades A-F (A=4..F=0). Overall = weighted average, but ANY autoDisqualifier floors the overall to F. Weights put 70% on the three skill axes (persistence/anticipation/regime-robustness), 30% on deflated-Sharpe quality + blow-up safety.

neutralForUnmeasured._doc: Anticipation is unmeasured for most of the 1063 (fills coverage). Score unmeasured anticipation as C (2.0), NOT F — the tool's job is to SURFACE candidates for manual fill-mirroring review, not to bury everyone the study could not label.

autoDisqualifiers._doc: Any TRUE => overall grade F + 'DISQUALIFIED' flag. These encode the study's hard lessons: blow-ups, lottery profiles, and rank that inverts at every break.

thresholds._doc: Machine-readable disqualifier cutoffs the scorer evaluates (JSON is the source of truth). studyPeriodReturnBelow / maxDrawdownFracAbove / worstWindowReturnBelow / topDecileForwardCountBelow are simple; the lottery rule trips when sharpeAnnual < lotterySharpeBelow AND pnlConcentration > lotteryConcentrationAbove; the fragility rule trips when regimeBreakSurvival == 0 AND topDecileForwardCount < fragileTopDecileBelow.

overall._doc: Emit BOTH a letter and a 0-10 score per category and overall. A disqualified wallet shows F/0 overall with the breached rule(s) listed. Default shortlist = overall grade >= B.

uiHints._doc: For the trader-browser UI: this tool surfaces RARE NEEDLES for MANUAL review (copy path is fill-mirroring, not vaults — none of the 43 run vaults). Make persistence/anticipation/regime columns primary; demote raw PnL to a context column with a caution tooltip.

cautionBadges._doc: Surface the study's honest caveats inline so a human does not over-trust the grade.
cautionBadges.ANTICIPATION_UNMEASURED: fills coverage insufficient; entry-timing edge not verified
cautionBadges.NO_VAULT_PATH: not copyable via vaults; requires fill-mirroring infra
cautionBadges.REGIME_BREAK_RISK: rank inverts at regime breaks; a copy made after a hot streak may catch the inversion
